discovery.run_summary: Modestly risk-on tape with SPY/QQQ up and VIX easing on US-Iran de-escalation headlines; small caps (IWM) leading and semis (SMH) strong despite NVDA weakness. Movers list is thin and notably lacks 8-K catalysts — most flagged names appear to be sympathy/momentum moves rather than news-driven, with one exception (BMI Reg-FD filing).

notable_index_moves: IWM +0.93% leading, consistent with broadening narrative referenced in MarketWatch S&P 493 piece
XLE +0.61% holding up despite oil tumbling on Iran peace progress — worth watching for reversal
SMH +1.49% strong even as NVDA -1.9%, suggesting rotation within semis (broadening beyond AI leader)
XLC -0.55% the lone red sector, dragged by GOOGL -1.21%

discoveries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Mid-cap semi extending a multi-day rally with no visible company catalyst
discoveries.thesis: Move has now compounded to a significant multi-day run without an 8-K or earnings event. Could be activist/M&A speculation (QRVO has been a recurring activist target), sympathy with broader semi rotation away from NVDA into smaller chip names (SMH strong, NVDA weak), or leaked sell-side note. Without a visible driver, direction-from-here is genuinely uncertain.
discoveries.what_confirms: An 8-K from QRVO disclosing a strategic review, takeover approach, or large design win; or sell-side upgrade tied to Apple/Samsung RF content gains.
discoveries.what_kills: Discovery that the move is purely retail/momentum with no fundamental driver — would argue for fade. Alternatively, confirmed activist 13D would invalidate the 'no catalyst' framing entirely.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a non-AI semi rallies hard in a tape where SMH is up but NVDA is down, the rotation-within-semis hypothesis deserves explicit checking before defaulting to sympathy.
discoveries.catalyst: No 8-K, no earnings within the window. Possibly sympathy with semi rotation or activist/M&A chatter (Starboard has historically held QRVO).
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty {}; SMH +1.49% while NVDA -1.9% is consistent with mid-cap semi rotation.
research_pointers: Scan for sell-side notes published today on RF semis / Apple supply chain
Look for any M&A rumor coverage (QRVO has been recurring takeout speculation)
Check options flow for unusual call activity suggesting leaked info

discoveries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Quality compounder continuing run-up after Reg-FD 8-K disclosure
discoveries.thesis: BMI filed a 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure on May 21 and the stock has continued higher into today. Reg-FD filings can be either bullish (investor day reaffirming/raising outlook) or neutral (boilerplate conference deck). Without knowing the content of the filing, I cannot articulate a direction at conf 3+. BMI is a low-beta water-meter compounder that rarely moves this much without substance, which slightly biases bullish, but not enough to commit.
discoveries.what_confirms: The 7.01 exhibit contains raised guidance, a large municipal contract win, or a positive investor-day update.
discoveries.what_kills: The 7.01 is routine investor conference boilerplate — in which case the move is sentiment/sympathy with the utility/water infrastructure theme and could fade.
discoveries.catalyst: May 21 8-K with 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure (content unknown from filing codes alone).
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 7.01 + 9.01 filing on 5/21 precedes the multi-day strength; code alone cannot determine bullish vs. neutral.
research_pointers: Read the actual 8-K exhibit to see if it contained guidance or a contract announcement
Check if BMI presented at a conference 5/21 and whether commentary was upbeat
Compare move to peer XYL, ROP water/instrumentation names — sympathy or BMI-specific?

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Defense small/mid-cap moving with sector on geopolitical news flow
discoveries.thesis: Move is consistent with ITA +0.99% and ongoing defense procurement narrative (drones/loitering munitions remain a priority regardless of any Iran de-escalation — Ukraine/Pacific demand persists). Magnitude is larger than the sector ETF but AVAV is high-beta within defense and is a pure-play on the drone theme that has been a structural winner. No 8-K, no manufactured thesis warranted — the move looks proportionate to ongoing tailwinds rather than mispriced.
discoveries.what_confirms: Defense ETF continues to lead and AVAV outperforms peers KTOS, RTX over coming sessions on drone-specific catalysts.
discoveries.what_kills: An Iran peace deal genuinely reduces defense spending expectations and AVAV gives back gains — would suggest the move was actually overdone and tied to the Iran narrative inverse to what I'm reading.
discoveries.catalyst: Sector strength in defense names (ITA +0.99%); AVAV is high-beta within that group. No company-specific catalyst visible.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, but ITA +0.99% confirms sector context.
research_pointers: Check for any DoD contract announcements or foreign military sales news on drones
Watch AVAV upcoming earnings calendar — fiscal Q4 typically reports in late June
Monitor Iran-deal headlines for inverse sensitivity in defense names

discoveries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Networking small-cap spiking on below-average volume with no catalyst
discoveries.thesis: Volume multiple is below 1x (0.89), which is unusual for a sharp single-day move and weakens the conviction that institutional buyers are accumulating on news. Could be sympathy with networking peers (ANET, CSCO) on AI-networking theme but neither moved sharply today. No 8-K. The combination of sharp move + below-average volume + no visible catalyst is a yellow flag — could be a thin-tape squeeze rather than informed buying.
discoveries.what_confirms: Follow-through on heavier volume tomorrow, or a delayed 8-K/news announcement explaining the move.
discoveries.what_kills: Volume stays light and the move fades over the next 1-2 sessions — classic thin-tape pop pattern.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Sharp price moves on below-average volume are a distinct pattern from sharp moves on heavy volume — the former more often fades, the latter more often signals real information flow.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown. Possibly networking sympathy or short squeeze.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals plus volume_multiple of 0.89 — informed accumulation typically shows >1.5x.
research_pointers: Check short interest and days-to-cover for squeeze potential
Look for any Gartner Magic Quadrant updates or enterprise networking news
Compare to ANET, JNPR, CSCO for sympathy confirmation

discovery.no_signals_note: Thin discovery list with three of four movers having no catalyst_signals at all. Highest-quality flag is BMI (actual 8-K to read), but Reg-FD code alone doesn't determine direction. Honest output: pointers for further research, not directional calls.

ai_announcements.headline: ClickUp mass layoffs replacing hundreds with AI agents
ai_announcements.summary: Productivity SaaS startup ClickUp laying off staff, replacing with AI agents. Signals broader 'agents replace workers' theme but ClickUp is private.

ai_announcements.headline: Pope Leo XIV AI encyclical
ai_announcements.summary: Philosophical/ethical critique of AI concentration of power. No direct corporate impact.

ai_announcements.headline: AI security navigated in real time, even at Google
ai_announcements.summary: Commentary piece on AI security challenges. No specific product announcement.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: None of the moving stocks (QRVO, AVAV, EXTR, BMI) have plausible linkage to today's AI news items. QRVO (RF semis) and AVAV (defense drones) moves are likely M&A/defense-budget driven, not AI-news driven. EXTR (networking) and BMI (water meters) similarly disconnected from ClickUp/Pope/Google-security headlines. The ClickUp story is the only directionally interesting AI labor-substitution datapoint, but ClickUp is private and no listed productivity-SaaS peer (MNDY, ASAN, SMAR) appears in the movers list. Quiet night for the AI-selloff thesis.
